cards.definition: family — los padres (parents), los hermanos (siblings), los abuelos (grandparents)
marriage — casarse (to get married), la boda (wedding), el marido/la mujer (husband/wife)
divorce — divorciarse (to get divorced), separado/a (separated)
friendship — un/a amigo/a (a friend), llevarse bien con (to get on well with)
relationships — salir con (to go out with), enamorarse de (to fall in love with)
household chores — fregar los platos (do the dishes), pasar la aspiradora (vacuum), hacer la cama (make the bed)
breakfast — in Spain often a light meal — café con leche, tostadas
lunch — the main meal in Spain, usually eaten between 2–4pm
dinner — a lighter evening meal, usually eaten late (9–11pm in Spain)
daily routine — levantarse (get up), acostarse (go to bed), ducharse (have a shower)

questions.question: What does 'los padres' mean?
questions.explanation: Los padres = parents. Los abuelos = grandparents. Los hermanos = siblings.

questions.question: What does 'casarse' mean?
questions.explanation: Casarse = to get married. Me caso el año que viene = I am getting married next year.

questions.question: What does 'llevarse bien con' mean?
options: D) To get on well with
questions.explanation: Llevarse bien con = to get on well with. Me llevo bien con mi hermano = I get on well with my brother.

questions.question: What does 'fregar los platos' mean?
questions.explanation: Fregar los platos = to do the dishes. Also lavar los platos. Fregar = to scrub/wash.

questions.question: What is 'la comida' in the Spanish meal schedule?
options: C) Lunch — the main meal in Spain
questions.explanation: La comida / el almuerzo = lunch — the main meal of the day in Spain, eaten between 2–4pm.

questions.question: What does 'acostarse' mean?
questions.explanation: Acostarse = to go to bed. Levantarse = to get up. Both are reflexive verbs.

questions.question: What is 'la amistad'?
questions.explanation: La amistad = friendship. Un/a amigo/a = a friend. Mi mejor amigo/a = my best friend.

questions.question: What does 'salir con' mean?
options: A) To go out from a place
B) To go out with (in a relationship)
questions.explanation: Salir con = to go out with / to date. Salgo con alguien = I am going out with someone.

questions.question: What is 'la cena'?
questions.explanation: La cena = dinner. In Spain, dinner is typically eaten late — often 9–11pm.

questions.question: What does 'ducharse' mean?
questions.explanation: Ducharse = to have a shower. Bañarse = to have a bath. Both are reflexive verbs.

questions.question: Los padres means grandparents in Spanish.
questions.explanation: Los padres = parents. Los abuelos = grandparents.

questions.question: Casarse means to get divorced.
questions.explanation: Casarse = to get married. Divorciarse = to get divorced.

questions.question: Llevarse bien con means to get on well with.
questions.explanation: Me llevo bien con mis padres = I get on well with my parents.

questions.question: La comida is the main meal of the day in Spain.
questions.explanation: In Spain, la comida (lunch) is eaten 2–4pm and is traditionally the largest meal.

questions.question: Fregar los platos means to make the bed.
questions.explanation: Fregar los platos = to do the dishes. Hacer la cama = to make the bed.

questions.question: Acostarse means to get up in the morning.
questions.explanation: Acostarse = to go to bed. Levantarse = to get up.

questions.question: La amistad means friendship.
questions.explanation: La amistad = friendship. Un/a amigo/a = a friend.

questions.question: Salir con means to go shopping.
questions.explanation: Salir con = to go out with (in a relationship). Ir de compras = to go shopping.

questions.question: La cena in Spain is typically eaten late in the evening.
questions.explanation: Spanish dinner (la cena) is usually eaten between 9pm and 11pm.

questions.question: Ducharse means to have a shower.
questions.explanation: Ducharse = to have a shower. Me ducho cada mañana = I shower every morning.

questions.question: La ________ = family.
questions.explanation: La familia incluye los padres, los hermanos y los abuelos.

questions.question: ________ = to get married.
questions.explanation: Me gustaría casarme algún día = I would like to get married someday.

questions.question: Llevarse ________ con = to get on well with.
questions.explanation: Me llevo bien con mis compañeros de clase = I get on well with my classmates.

questions.question: Fregar los ________ = to do the dishes.
questions.explanation: Las tareas domésticas = household chores.

questions.question: La ________ = lunch — the main meal in Spain.
questions.explanation: En España, la comida es la comida principal del día.

questions.question: ________ = to go to bed.
questions.explanation: Me acuesto a las diez = I go to bed at ten.

questions.question: La ________ = friendship.
questions.explanation: La amistad es muy importante para mí = Friendship is very important to me.

questions.question: ________ con = to go out with (in a relationship).
questions.explanation: Salgo con alguien desde hace tres meses = I've been going out with someone for three months.

questions.question: La ________ = dinner — eaten late in Spain.
questions.explanation: La cena en España se sirve tarde, a veces a las once = Dinner in Spain is served late, sometimes at 11.

questions.question: ________ = to have a shower (reflexive verb).
questions.explanation: Me ducho por la mañana = I have a shower in the morning.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.

cards.definition: the present tense — hablar: hablo, hablas, habla, hablamos, habláis, hablan
the preterite — completed past actions — hablé (I spoke), fui (I went), comí (I ate)
the imperfect — ongoing/habitual past — hablaba (I was speaking/used to speak)
near future — ir a + infinitive — voy a hablar (I am going to speak)
simple future — hablaré (I will speak), iré (I will go)
conditional — would — me gustaría (I would like), hablaría (I would speak)
reflexive verbs — action done to yourself — levantarse, ducharse, llamarse
two verbs meaning 'to be' — ser for permanent qualities; estar for temporary states and location
adjectives — must agree in gender and number — grande, grandes, pequeño/a
negation — no (not), nunca (never), nada (nothing), nadie (nobody), tampoco (neither)

questions.question: How do you form the futuro inmediato?
options: D) Present tense of querer + infinitive
questions.explanation: Futuro inmediato = ir a + infinitive. Voy a estudiar = I am going to study.

questions.question: What is the difference between ser and estar?
options: B) Ser is for places; estar is for people
C) Ser for permanent qualities; estar for temporary states and location
D) Ser is used in the past; estar in the present
questions.explanation: Ser: permanent (nationality, character, profession). Estar: temporary (emotions, health, location). Soy inglés / Estoy cansado.

questions.question: What is the preterite (pretérito indefinido) used for?
questions.explanation: The preterite is for completed single past actions: Fui al cine = I went to the cinema. Comí = I ate.

questions.question: What does 'me gustaría' mean?
questions.explanation: Me gustaría = I would like. It is the conditional of gustar — used for polite requests and preferences.

questions.question: How does 'gustar' work in Spanish?
options: A) Like English — yo gusto el chocolate
B) The thing liked is the subject — me gusta el chocolate
C) It uses ser as auxiliary
questions.explanation: Gustar works backwards: me gusta (I like — lit. 'it pleases me'). Me gustan las películas (I like films — plural).

questions.question: What is the imperfect (imperfecto) used for?
options: C) Ongoing/habitual past actions and descriptions
questions.explanation: The imperfect: Cuando era joven, jugaba al fútbol = When I was young, I used to play football. Also for descriptions.

questions.question: What does 'nunca' mean?
questions.explanation: Nunca = never. No voy nunca al cine = I never go to the cinema. (Double negative is correct in Spanish.)

questions.question: What is the rule for adjective agreement in Spanish?
options: B) Adjectives agree in gender and number with the noun
D) Adjectives only change for plural
questions.explanation: Spanish adjectives agree: alto (m sg) → alta (f sg) → altos (m pl) → altas (f pl).

questions.question: How do reflexive verbs work?
options: A) They use haber as auxiliary
C) The subject does the action to themselves — levantarse, ducharse
D) They only exist in the past tense
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs use reflexive pronouns: me, te, se, nos, os, se. Me levanto = I get up.

questions.question: What is the futur simple of 'hablar'?
questions.explanation: Futur simple: add endings to infinitive — hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablaréis, hablarán.

questions.question: Futuro inmediato uses ir a + infinitive.
questions.explanation: Voy a visitar España el año que viene = I am going to visit Spain next year.

questions.question: Ser and estar are interchangeable in Spanish.
questions.explanation: Ser is for permanent characteristics; estar is for temporary states and location. They cannot be swapped.

questions.question: The pretérito indefinido is used for completed single past actions.
questions.explanation: Ayer fui al parque = Yesterday I went to the park.

questions.question: Me gustaría means 'I like'.
questions.explanation: Me gustaría = I would like (conditional). Me gusta = I like (present).

questions.question: Nunca requires the word 'no' before the verb in Spanish.
questions.explanation: No voy nunca = I never go. Spanish uses double negatives — both no and nunca are required.

questions.question: Spanish adjectives agree in gender and number with the noun.
questions.explanation: Adjectives change form: alto/alta/altos/altas depending on the noun's gender and number.

questions.question: Gustar works like English — yo gusto el chocolate.
questions.explanation: Gustar works backwards: me gusta el chocolate (I like chocolate). The thing liked is the subject.

questions.question: The imperfect is used for completed single events.
questions.explanation: The imperfect is for ongoing/habitual past. The preterite is for completed single events.

questions.question: Reflexive verbs use the pronouns me, te, se, nos, os, se.
questions.explanation: Me levanto, te duchas, se llama — reflexive pronouns precede the conjugated verb.

questions.question: The futuro simple of hablar is hablaré.
questions.explanation: Hablaré (I will speak), hablarás (you will speak), hablará (he/she will speak).

questions.question: Futuro inmediato = ________ a + infinitive.
questions.explanation: Voy a comer = I am going to eat. Van a salir = They are going to go out.

questions.question: ________ is for permanent qualities; estar is for temporary states.
questions.explanation: Soy español = I am Spanish (permanent). Estoy cansado = I am tired (temporary).

questions.question: The ________ indefinido is used for completed past actions.
questions.explanation: Fui al cine ayer = I went to the cinema yesterday.

questions.question: Me ________ = I would like (conditional of gustar).
questions.explanation: Me gustaría visitar España = I would like to visit Spain.

questions.question: ________ = never. (Double negative required in Spanish.)
questions.explanation: No hago nunca mi cama = I never make my bed.

questions.question: Spanish adjectives agree in ________ and number with the noun.
questions.explanation: Alto/alta/altos/altas depending on the noun described.

questions.question: Me ________ = I like (present — the thing liked is the subject).
questions.explanation: Me gusta el fútbol. Me gustan las películas. (Gusta = singular; gustan = plural.)

questions.question: The ________ is used for ongoing or habitual past actions.
questions.explanation: Cuando era niño, comía mucho chocolate = When I was a child, I used to eat lots of chocolate.

questions.question: ________ verbs use pronouns me, te, se — the subject does the action to themselves.
questions.explanation: Levantarse, ducharse, llamarse, acostarse.

questions.question: Futuro simple of -ar verbs = infinitive + ________.
questions.explanation: Hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablaréis, hablarán.

questions.instruction: Match each term to its correct description.
